action.reasoning: Observation: v21 yields 0 safe_steps across 5 test eps (max_streak=0), detector triggers occasionally (up to 136 steps) but never satisfies both dist in [1,2) AND |angle|<0.2618 simultaneously; avg_dist~5.6m, found_rate low, eval safe_follow~11.7. Earlier v4-v5 reached ~50 avg safe. Config thresholds exact (500 accumulated, success_angle=0.2618rad/15deg, facing=0.5). Analysis: follow-mode gains cause overshoot/oscillation on dynamic oracle (human speed ~0.25-0.35m/s not matched); OA interferes near human; seek not persistent enough to close gap often. Root cause is imperfect stabilization when near (linear/err term + angle threshold). Reactive heuristic still best path in budget (SPF navmesh issues persist). Decision: rewrite COMPLETE non-truncated test_full_episode.py + predict.py with v22 (tighter follow: err=dist-1.5, base_linear=0.22, gain=0.08 clipped [0.1,0.4], prioritize pure-rotate if |angle|>0.25 (tighter than success_angle), reduced OA sensitivity (min_front<0.9 + detector<0.2), detector>0.1 boosts follow, aligned vel=0.22, backup<0.85, robust depth valid>0.05 with center 40% for min_front, per-step accumulated safe count + streak/max_streak/detector/collisions inside loop using exact policy attrs, fixed collision dict handling, full JSON contract + make_serializable + per_episode with all keys + metrics fallback, abs paths + dataset override). Update train.py to v22. Run test_full_episode.py to validate improved safe/streak/detector/avg_dist before submit_eval on predict.py (dummy ckpt). Target: >100 avg safe + first >500 in lucky epi for positive success.
